rationale: The citing paper gives Rutz and Bucklin a dedicated discussion in the sponsored-search literature review, summarizing its generic-to-branded spillover mechanism and using it to position a distinct research gap on ad position, revenue, and profit. The authors explicitly argue why that prior study does not answer their focal question (limited relevance of ad position for branded terms) and then run their own spillover robustness analysis in response to that stream. This is more than a passing mention because it helps define scope boundaries and motivates part of the empirical checks.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Rutz and Bucklin as a key prior study on spillover in paid search, then differentiates its own contribution by focusing on ad-position profitability rather than branded-keyword spillovers. It incorporates the spillover concept as a robustness check and reports that spillover value is not position-dependent in its setting.
